title: Vector Algebra

sections.title: Introduction
concepts: Scalar quantities: quantities involving only one value (magnitude), e.g., length, mass, time, distance, speed, area, volume, temperature, work, money, voltage, density, resistance
Vector quantities: quantities involving both magnitude and direction, e.g., displacement, velocity, acceleration, force, weight, momentum, electric field intensity
Vectors have algebraic and geometric properties that together give a full realisation of the concept
key_points: Scalars are real numbers representing magnitude only
Vectors have both magnitude and direction
This chapter covers basic concepts, operations on vectors, and their algebraic and geometric properties

sections.title: Some Basic Concepts
concepts: Directed line: a line given a direction by means of arrowheads
Directed line segment: a line segment with magnitude and direction
A vector is denoted as AB (with arrow) or a (with arrow), read as 'vector AB' or 'vector a'
Initial point: the point A from where the vector AB starts
Terminal point: the point B where the vector AB ends
Magnitude (or length) of a vector: the distance between initial and terminal points, denoted |AB|, |a|, or a
The arrow indicates the direction of the vector
Position vector: vector OP having origin O as initial point and point P as terminal point
Direction angles: angles alpha, beta, gamma made by a vector with the positive directions of x, y, z axes
Direction cosines: cos(alpha), cos(beta), cos(gamma) denoted by l, m, n respectively
Direction ratios: numbers lr, mr, nr (proportional to direction cosines), denoted as a, b, c

definitions.definition: A quantity that has magnitude as well as direction is called a vector. A directed line segment is a vector.
The vector OP having O (origin) as initial point and P as terminal point is called the position vector of point P with respect to origin O.
The cosine values of the direction angles alpha, beta, gamma that a vector makes with the positive x, y, z axes, denoted by l, m, n respectively.
Numbers proportional to the direction cosines of a vector, denoted as a, b, c.

formulas.name: Direction cosines
formulas.formula: cos(alpha) = x/r, cos(beta) = y/r, cos(gamma) = z/r where r = |r_vec|

key_points: Since the length is never negative, the notation |a| < 0 has no meaning
l^2 + m^2 + n^2 = 1 but a^2 + b^2 + c^2 != 1 in general
Coordinates of point P(x,y,z) can be expressed as (lr, mr, nr)

sections.title: Types of Vectors

definitions.definition: A vector whose initial and terminal points coincide, denoted as 0_vec. It has zero magnitude and cannot be assigned a definite direction (or may be regarded as having any direction). Vectors AA, BB represent the zero vector.
A vector whose magnitude is unity (1 unit). The unit vector in the direction of a given vector a is denoted by a_hat.
Two or more vectors having the same initial point are called coinitial vectors.
Two or more vectors are said to be collinear if they are parallel to the same line, irrespective of their magnitudes and directions.
Two vectors a and b are said to be equal if they have the same magnitude and direction regardless of the positions of their initial points, written as a = b.
A vector whose magnitude is the same as that of a given vector but direction is opposite to that of it. Vector BA is negative of vector AB, written as BA = -AB.
Vectors that may be subject to parallel displacement without changing magnitude and direction. Throughout this chapter, we deal with free vectors only.

key_points: Zero vector has zero magnitude and indeterminate direction
Unit vector a_hat has |a_hat| = 1
Coinitial vectors share the same starting point
Collinear vectors are parallel to the same line
Equal vectors have same magnitude AND same direction
Negative vector has same magnitude but opposite direction

sections.title: Addition of Vectors
concepts: Properties of vector addition: commutative and associative

definitions.definition: If two vectors a and b are positioned so that the initial point of one coincides with the terminal point of the other, then the sum (resultant) a + b is represented by the third side of the triangle formed. In triangle ABC: AC = AB + BC.

If two vectors a and b are represented by the two adjacent sides of a parallelogram in magnitude and direction, then their sum a + b is represented in magnitude and direction by the diagonal of the parallelogram through their common point.

formulas.name: Vector difference
formulas.formula: a - b = AB + BC' where BC' = -BC

formulas.name: Sides of triangle sum to zero

theorems.name: Commutative Property of Vector Addition
theorems.statement: For any two vectors a and b: a + b = b + a
theorems.proof_outline: Consider parallelogram ABCD with AB = a and BC = b. Using triangle law on triangles ABC and ADC, AC = a + b = b + a.

theorems.name: Associative Property of Vector Addition
theorems.statement: For any three vectors a, b, c: (a + b) + c = a + (b + c)
theorems.proof_outline: Let a, b, c be represented by PQ, QR, RS respectively. Then (a+b)+c = PR + RS = PS and a+(b+c) = PQ + QS = PS.

key_points: The triangle law and parallelogram law of vector addition are equivalent
When sides of a triangle are taken in order, the resultant is zero (initial and terminal points coincide)
a + 0 = 0 + a = a (zero vector is the additive identity)
The associative property allows writing a + b + c without brackets
The two laws of vector addition are equivalent to each other

sections.title: Multiplication of a Vector by a Scalar
concepts: Unit vectors along axes: i_hat, j_hat, k_hat

definitions.definition: The product of vector a by scalar lambda, denoted lambda*a, is a vector collinear to a. The vector lambda*a has direction same (or opposite) to a according as lambda is positive (or negative). Its magnitude is |lambda| times the magnitude of a: |lambda*a| = |lambda||a|.

Any vector r = xi_hat + yj_hat + zk_hat is said to be in component form. Here x, y, z are scalar components and xi_hat, yj_hat, zk_hat are vector components along the respective axes. Also called rectangular components.
The position vector of point R dividing the line segment PQ internally in the ratio m:n is OR = (mb + na)/(m + n), where a and b are position vectors of P and Q.
The position vector of point R dividing the line segment PQ externally in the ratio m:n is OR = (mb - na)/(m - n).

formulas.name: Sum of vectors in component form
Difference of vectors in component form
Equality of vectors
formulas.formula: a = b if and only if a1 = b1, a2 = b2, a3 = b3

formulas.name: Collinearity condition
formulas.formula: a and b are collinear iff b = lambda*a for some nonzero scalar lambda, i.e., b1/a1 = b2/a2 = b3/a3 = lambda

key_points: When lambda = -1, lambda*a = -a (the negative/additive inverse of vector a)
lambda = 1/|a| gives unit vector in the direction of a
i_hat, j_hat, k_hat are unit vectors along x, y, z axes with |OA| = |OB| = |OC| = 1
Two vectors are collinear iff one is a scalar multiple of the other
Components a1, a2, a3 of a = a1*i_hat + a2*j_hat + a3*k_hat are also called direction ratios of a
If l, m, n are direction cosines, then l*i_hat + m*j_hat + n*k_hat is the unit vector in that direction
If R is the midpoint of PQ then m = n, giving OR = (a + b)/2

sections.title: Product of Two Vectors
concepts: Projection of a vector on a line

definitions.definition: The scalar product of two nonzero vectors a and b, denoted by a . b, is defined as a . b = |a||b|cos(theta), where theta is the angle between a and b, 0 <= theta <= pi. If either a = 0 or b = 0, then theta is not defined and a . b = 0.

The vector product of two nonzero vectors a and b, denoted by a x b, is defined as a x b = |a||b|sin(theta) * n_hat, where theta is the angle between a and b (0 <= theta <= pi) and n_hat is a unit vector perpendicular to both a and b such that a, b, n_hat form a right-handed system.
If vector AB makes angle theta with directed line l, the projection of AB on l is a vector p with magnitude |AB||cos(theta)| and direction same as (or opposite to) the line l.

formulas.name: Dot product of perpendicular vectors
formulas.formula: a . b = 0 if and only if a is perpendicular to b (for nonzero vectors)

formulas.name: Dot product when theta = 0
formulas.formula: a . b = |a||b|; in particular a . a = |a|^2

formulas.name: Dot products of unit vectors
formulas.formula: i_hat . i_hat = j_hat . j_hat = k_hat . k_hat = 1; i_hat . j_hat = j_hat . k_hat = k_hat . i_hat = 0

formulas.name: Angle between vectors using dot product
formulas.formula: cos(theta) = (a . b)/(|a||b|), or theta = cos^(-1)((a . b)/(|a||b|))

formulas.name: Distributive property of dot product
formulas.formula: a . (b + c) = a . b + a . c

formulas.name: Projection of a on b
formulas.formula: Projection of a on b = (a . b)/|b| = a . b_hat

formulas.name: Projection of a on b (vector form)
Direction cosines from dot product
formulas.formula: cos(alpha) = a1/|a|, cos(beta) = a2/|a|, cos(gamma) = a3/|a|

formulas.name: Unit vector in terms of direction cosines
Cross product of parallel/collinear vectors
formulas.formula: a x b = 0_vec if and only if a is parallel to b (for nonzero vectors)

formulas.name: Cross products of unit vectors
formulas.formula: i_hat x i_hat = j_hat x j_hat = k_hat x k_hat = 0_vec; i_hat x j_hat = k_hat, j_hat x k_hat = i_hat, k_hat x i_hat = j_hat

formulas.name: Reverse cross products of unit vectors
formulas.formula: j_hat x i_hat = -k_hat, k_hat x j_hat = -i_hat, i_hat x k_hat = -j_hat

formulas.name: Area of triangle
formulas.formula: Area = (1/2)|a x b| where a, b are adjacent sides

formulas.name: Area of parallelogram
formulas.formula: Area = |a x b| where a, b are adjacent sides

formulas.name: Distributive property of cross product
formulas.formula: a x (b + c) = a x b + a x c

formulas.name: Scalar factor in cross product
formulas.formula: lambda(a x b) = (lambda*a) x b = a x (lambda*b)

formulas.name: Cross product in component form (determinant)
formulas.formula: a x b = |i_hat  j_hat  k_hat; a1  a2  a3; b1  b2  b3| = (a2*b3 - a3*b2)i_hat - (a1*b3 - a3*b1)j_hat + (a1*b2 - a2*b1)k_hat

theorems.name: Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality
theorems.statement: For any two vectors a and b: |a . b| <= |a||b|
theorems.proof_outline: Since |a . b|/(|a||b|) = |cos(theta)| <= 1, it follows that |a . b| <= |a||b|.

theorems.name: Triangle Inequality
theorems.statement: For any two vectors a and b: |a + b| <= |a| + |b|

theorems.name: Collinearity from Triangle Inequality
theorems.statement: If |a + b| = |a| + |b|, then |AC| = |AB| + |BC|, showing that A, B, C are collinear.

key_points: a . b is a real number (scalar), not a vector
a . b = 0 iff a is perpendicular to b (for nonzero vectors)
a x b is a vector, not a scalar
a x b = 0_vec iff a is parallel to b (for nonzero vectors)
Dot product is commutative: a . b = b . a
Cross product is NOT commutative: a x b = -(b x a) (anti-commutative)
Both dot and cross products are distributive over addition
If theta = 0 or pi, projection vector of AB is AB itself or BA
If theta = pi/2 or 3pi/2, projection vector of AB is zero vector
The projections of a along OX, OY, OZ are the scalar components a1, a2, a3
If a is a unit vector, a_hat = cos(alpha)*i_hat + cos(beta)*j_hat + cos(gamma)*k_hat
There are two perpendicular directions to any plane, so the unit vector perpendicular to a+b and a-b can also be the negative of the one found
Cross product gives area interpretation: triangle = (1/2)|a x b|, parallelogram = |a x b|

summary: Position vector of a point P(x, y, z) is given as OP = r = xi_hat + yj_hat + zk_hat, and its magnitude by sqrt(x^2 + y^2 + z^2).
The scalar components of a vector are its direction ratios, and represent its projections along the respective axes.
The magnitude (r), direction ratios (a, b, c) and direction cosines (l, m, n) of any vector are related as: l = a/r, m = b/r, n = c/r.
The vector sum of the three sides of a triangle taken in order is 0_vec.
The vector sum of two coinitial vectors is given by the diagonal of the parallelogram whose adjacent sides are the given vectors.

The multiplication of a given vector by a scalar lambda changes the magnitude of the vector by the multiple |lambda|, and keeps the direction same (or makes it opposite) according as the value of lambda is positive (or negative).
For a given vector a, the vector a_hat = a/|a| gives the unit vector in the direction of a.

The position vector of a point R dividing a line segment joining the points P and Q whose position vectors are a and b respectively, in the ratio m : n: (i) internally, is given by (na + mb)/(m + n); (ii) externally, is given by (mb - na)/(m - n).

The scalar product of two given vectors a and b having angle theta between them is defined as a . b = |a||b|cos(theta). Also, when a . b is given, the angle theta between the vectors a and b may be determined by cos(theta) = (a . b)/(|a||b|).

If theta is the angle between two vectors a and b, then their cross product is given as a x b = |a||b|sin(theta) * n_hat, where n_hat is a unit vector perpendicular to the plane containing a and b, such that a, b, n_hat form right handed system of coordinate axes.

If we have two vectors a and b, given in component form as a = a1*i_hat + a2*j_hat + a3*k_hat and b = b1*i_hat + b2*j_hat + b3*k_hat and lambda any scalar, then: a + b = (a1+b1)*i_hat + (a2+b2)*j_hat + (a3+b3)*k_hat; lambda*a = (lambda*a1)*i_hat + (lambda*a2)*j_hat + (lambda*a3)*k_hat; a . b = a1*b1 + a2*b2 + a3*b3; a x b = |i_hat j_hat k_hat; a1 a2 a3; b1 b2 b3| (determinant form).

questions.question: Represent graphically a displacement of 40 km, 30 degrees east of north.

Classify the following measures as scalars and vectors.
(i) 10 kg
(ii) 2 meters north-west
(iii) 40 degrees
(iv) 40 watt
(v) 10^(-19) coulomb
(vi) 20 m/s^2

Classify the following as scalar and vector quantities.
(i) time period
(ii) distance
(iii) force
(iv) velocity
(v) work done

In Fig 10.6 (a square), identify the following vectors.
(i) Coinitial
(ii) Equal
(iii) Collinear but not equal

Answer the following as true or false.
(i) a and -a are collinear.
(ii) Two collinear vectors are always equal in magnitude.
(iii) Two vectors having same magnitude are collinear.
(iv) Two collinear vectors having the same magnitude are equal.
Write two different vectors having same magnitude.
Write two different vectors having same direction.
Find the values of x and y so that the vectors 2i_hat + 3j_hat and xi_hat + yj_hat are equal.
Find the scalar and vector components of the vector with initial point (2, 1) and terminal point (-5, 7).
Find the sum of the vectors a = i_hat - 2j_hat + k_hat, b = -2i_hat + 4j_hat + 5k_hat and c = i_hat - 6j_hat - 7k_hat.
Find the unit vector in the direction of the vector a = i_hat + j_hat + 2k_hat.
Find the unit vector in the direction of vector PQ, where P and Q are the points (1, 2, 3) and (4, 5, 6), respectively.
For given vectors, a = 2i_hat - j_hat + 2k_hat and b = -i_hat + j_hat - k_hat, find the unit vector in the direction of the vector a + b.
Find a vector in the direction of vector 5i_hat - j_hat + 2k_hat which has magnitude 8 units.
Show that the vectors 2i_hat - 3j_hat + 4k_hat and -4i_hat + 6j_hat - 8k_hat are collinear.
Find the direction cosines of the vector i_hat + 2j_hat + 3k_hat.
Find the direction cosines of the vector joining the points A(1, 2, -3) and B(-1, -2, 1), directed from A to B.
Show that the vector i_hat + j_hat + k_hat is equally inclined to the axes OX, OY and OZ.

Find the position vector of a point R which divides the line joining two points P and Q whose position vectors are i_hat + 2j_hat - k_hat and -i_hat + j_hat + k_hat respectively, in the ratio 2 : 1
(i) internally
(ii) externally
Find the position vector of the mid point of the vector joining the points P(2, 3, 4) and Q(4, 1, -2).

Show that the points A, B and C with position vectors, a = 3i_hat - 4j_hat - 4k_hat, b = 2i_hat - j_hat + k_hat and c = i_hat - 3j_hat - 5k_hat, respectively form the vertices of a right angled triangle.

In triangle ABC (Fig 10.18), which of the following is not true:
(A) AB + BC + CA = 0_vec
(B) AB + BC - AC = 0_vec
(C) AB + BC - CA = 0_vec
(D) AB - CB + CA = 0_vec

If a and b are two collinear vectors, then which of the following are incorrect:
(A) b = lambda*a, for some scalar lambda
(B) a = +/- b
(C) the respective components of a and b are not proportional
(D) both the vectors a and b have same direction, but different magnitudes.
Find the angle between two vectors a and b with magnitudes sqrt(3) and 2, respectively having a . b = sqrt(6).
Find the angle between the vectors i_hat - 2j_hat + 3k_hat and 3i_hat - 2j_hat + k_hat.
Find the projection of the vector i_hat - j_hat on the vector i_hat + j_hat.
Find the projection of the vector i_hat + 3j_hat + 7k_hat on the vector 7i_hat - j_hat + 8k_hat.

Show that each of the given three vectors is a unit vector:
(1/7)(2i_hat + 3j_hat + 6k_hat), (1/7)(3i_hat - 6j_hat + 2k_hat), (1/7)(6i_hat + 2j_hat - 3k_hat)
Also, show that they are mutually perpendicular to each other.
Find |a| and |b|, if (a + b) . (a - b) = 8 and |a| = 8|b|.
Evaluate the product (3a - 5b) . (2a + 7b).
Find the magnitude of two vectors a and b, having the same magnitude and such that the angle between them is 60 degrees and their scalar product is 1/2.
Find |x|, if for a unit vector a, (x - a) . (x + a) = 12.
If a = 2i_hat + 2j_hat + 3k_hat, b = -i_hat + 2j_hat + k_hat and c = 3i_hat + j_hat are such that a + lambda*b is perpendicular to c, then find the value of lambda.
Show that |a|b + |b|a is perpendicular to |a|b - |b|a, for any two nonzero vectors a and b.
If a . a = 0 and a . b = 0, then what can be concluded about the vector b?
If a, b, c are unit vectors such that a + b + c = 0_vec, find the value of a . b + b . c + c . a.
If either vector a = 0_vec or b = 0_vec, then a . b = 0. But the converse need not be true. Justify your answer with an example.
If the vertices A, B, C of a triangle ABC are (1, 2, 3), (-1, 0, 0), (0, 1, 2), respectively, then find angle ABC [angle ABC is the angle between the vectors BA and BC].
Show that the points A(1, 2, 7), B(2, 6, 3) and C(3, 10, -1) are collinear.
Show that the vectors 2i_hat - j_hat + k_hat, i_hat - 3j_hat - 5k_hat and 3i_hat - 4j_hat - 4k_hat form the vertices of a right angled triangle.

If a is a nonzero vector of magnitude 'a' and lambda a nonzero scalar, then lambda*a is unit vector if
(A) lambda = 1
(B) lambda = -1
(C) a = |lambda|
(D) a = 1/|lambda|
Find |a x b|, if a = i_hat - 7j_hat + 7k_hat and b = 3i_hat - 2j_hat + 2k_hat.
Find a unit vector perpendicular to each of the vector a + b and a - b, where a = 3i_hat + 2j_hat + 2k_hat and b = i_hat + 2j_hat - 2k_hat.
If a unit vector a makes angles pi/3 with i_hat, pi/4 with j_hat and an acute angle theta with k_hat, then find theta and hence, the components of a.
Show that (a - b) x (a + b) = 2(a x b).
Find lambda and mu if (2i_hat + 6j_hat + 27k_hat) x (i_hat + lambda*j_hat + mu*k_hat) = 0_vec.
Given that a . b = 0 and a x b = 0_vec. What can you conclude about the vectors a and b?
Let the vectors a, b, c be given as a1*i_hat + a2*j_hat + a3*k_hat, b1*i_hat + b2*j_hat + b3*k_hat, c1*i_hat + c2*j_hat + c3*k_hat. Then show that a x (b + c) = a x b + a x c.
If either a = 0_vec or b = 0_vec, then a x b = 0_vec. Is the converse true? Justify your answer with an example.
Find the area of the triangle with vertices A(1, 1, 2), B(2, 3, 5) and C(1, 5, 5).
Find the area of the parallelogram whose adjacent sides are determined by the vectors a = i_hat - j_hat + 3k_hat and b = 2i_hat - 7j_hat + k_hat.

Let the vectors a and b be such that |a| = 3 and |b| = sqrt(2)/3, then a x b is a unit vector, if the angle between a and b is
(A) pi/6
(B) pi/4
(C) pi/3
(D) pi/2

Area of a rectangle having vertices A, B, C and D with position vectors -i_hat + (1/2)j_hat + 4k_hat, i_hat + (1/2)j_hat + 4k_hat, i_hat - (1/2)j_hat + 4k_hat and -i_hat - (1/2)j_hat + 4k_hat, respectively is
(A) 1/2
(B) 1
(C) 2
(D) 4
Write down a unit vector in XY-plane, making an angle of 30 degrees with the positive direction of x-axis.
Find the scalar components and magnitude of the vector joining the points P(x1, y1, z1) and Q(x2, y2, z2).
A girl walks 4 km towards west, then she walks 3 km in a direction 30 degrees east of north and stops. Determine the girl's displacement from her initial point of departure.
If a = b + c, then is it true that |a| = |b| + |c|? Justify your answer.
Find the value of x for which x(i_hat + j_hat + k_hat) is a unit vector.
Find a vector of magnitude 5 units, and parallel to the resultant of the vectors a = 2i_hat + 3j_hat - k_hat and b = i_hat - 2j_hat + k_hat.
If a = i_hat + j_hat + k_hat, b = 2i_hat - j_hat + 3k_hat and c = i_hat - 2j_hat + k_hat, find a unit vector parallel to the vector 2a - b + 3c.
Show that the points A(1, -2, -8), B(5, 0, -2) and C(11, 3, 7) are collinear, and find the ratio in which B divides AC.

Find the position vector of a point R which divides the line joining two points P and Q whose position vectors are (2a + b) and (a - 3b) externally in the ratio 1 : 2. Also, show that P is the mid point of the line segment RQ.
The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are 2i_hat - 4j_hat + 5k_hat and i_hat - 2j_hat - 3k_hat. Find the unit vector parallel to its diagonal. Also, find its area.
Show that the direction cosines of a vector equally inclined to the axes OX, OY and OZ are +/-(1/sqrt(3), 1/sqrt(3), 1/sqrt(3)).
Let a = i_hat + 4j_hat + 2k_hat, b = 3i_hat - 2j_hat + 7k_hat and c = 2i_hat - j_hat + 4k_hat. Find a vector d which is perpendicular to both a and b, and c . d = 15.

The scalar product of the vector i_hat + j_hat + k_hat with a unit vector along the sum of vectors 2i_hat + 4j_hat - 5k_hat and lambda*i_hat + 2j_hat + 3k_hat is equal to one. Find the value of lambda.
If a, b, c are mutually perpendicular vectors of equal magnitudes, show that the vector a + b + c is equally inclined to a, b and c.
Prove that (a + b) . (a + b) = |a|^2 + |b|^2, if and only if a, b are perpendicular, given a != 0_vec, b != 0_vec.

If theta is the angle between two vectors a and b, then a . b >= 0 only when
(A) 0 < theta < pi/2
(B) 0 <= theta <= pi/2
(C) 0 < theta < pi
(D) 0 <= theta <= pi

Let a and b be two unit vectors and theta is the angle between them. Then a + b is a unit vector if
(A) theta = pi/4
(B) theta = pi/3
(C) theta = pi/2
(D) theta = 2*pi/3

The value of i_hat . (j_hat x k_hat) + j_hat . (i_hat x k_hat) + k_hat . (i_hat x j_hat) is
(A) 0
(B) -1
(C) 1
(D) 3

If theta is the angle between any two vectors a and b, then |a . b| = |a x b| when theta is equal to
(A) 0
(B) pi/4
(C) pi/2
(D) pi
